source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/07_Retrofit (HnX-R1)/★ FDA 510(k) - K252912/NSE에 따른 PRE-SUB진행 (1차)/Clinical report/001_Clinical Image Report - G1717CW.docx

document.title: Clinical Image Report
standards: Guidance for the Submission of 510(k)s for Solid State X-ray Imaging Devices

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical image comparison report for G1717CW as part of the HnX-R1 digital X-ray imaging system, comparing it with predicate detector A1717MCW.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Used to support clinical adequacy and substantial equivalence of the Retrofit Kit of X-ray System (HnX-R1) for 510(k) submission purposes.
keyPoints: The report states that clinical image pairs were acquired using the subject device and a 510(k) cleared predicate device.
The subject device is G1717CW and the comparative or predicate device is A1717MCW.
The predicate device lists 510(k) Number K223930.
HnX-R1 is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
Images are saved or exported in DICOM 3.0 format and can be transmitted to PACS.
A qualified radiologist concluded that G1717CW images had diagnostic value and were slightly better in image quality in terms of spatial resolution and difference between substances.

concepts.name: Clinical image comparison for substantial equivalence
concepts.evidence: clinical image pairs were acquired using both the subject device and a 510(k) cleared predicate device. These images were evaluated and compared by a qualified expert (licensed radiologist)

concepts.name: Intended use for general radiographic diagnosis
concepts.evidence: The HnX-R1(Digital X-ray Imaging System) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Mammography exclusion
concepts.evidence: Not to be used for mammography.

concepts.name: DICOM 3.0 image format and export
concepts.evidence: Image formats, transmission, and storage supported by the DICOM 3.0 standard

concepts.name: PACS connectivity
concepts.evidence: The captured images are sent to the Picture Archiving & Communication System (PACS) server, and can be used for reading images.

concepts.name: Clinical expert qualification
concepts.evidence: The clinical evaluation of the subject device was conducted by a qualified medical specialist in the field of radiology.

evidence.claim: The document is titled Clinical Image Report.
The revision history identifies Revision No. 0 dated March 10, 2025 with description Initial.
evidence.evidenceText: Revision No. 0 Rev. Date March 10, 2025 Revision Description Initial

evidence.claim: The report purpose is to demonstrate clinical adequacy and substantial equivalence of the HnX-R1 retrofit kit.
evidence.evidenceText: This clinical image report is intended to demonstrate that the Retrofit Kit of X-ray System (HnX-R1) is clinically adequate for its intended use and is substantially equivalent to the predicate device.

evidence.claim: The reference cited is Section VII Clinical Considerations of the 510(k) guidance for solid state X-ray imaging devices.
evidence.evidenceText: Section VII Clinical Considerations of “Guidance for the Submission of 510(k)s for Solid State X-ray Imaging Devices.”
evidence.pageTarget: 510(k) solid state X-ray imaging device guidance

evidence.claim: The HnX-R1 system includes detector, power supply box, battery pack, battery charger, accessories, and image acquisition and processing software, and excludes the X-ray generator.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-R1 wired/wireless digital X-ray imaging system consists of Detector, Power supply box, Battery pack, Battery charger, Other Accessories and software for image acquisition and image processing and does not include the X-ray generator.
The name of the image acquisition and image processing software (Console software) is HnVUE.

evidence.claim: HnX-R1 is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of adult and child human anatomy and intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-R1(Digital X-ray Imaging System) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children. It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic system.

evidence.claim: HnX-R1 is not to be used for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The subject device used in the test was G1717CW with HnVUE version 1.0.0.
evidence.evidenceText: Subject device : Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector G1717CW ... Console software HnVUE Version: 1.0.0

evidence.claim: The predicate device lists 510(k) Number K223930 and detector A1717MCW.
evidence.evidenceText: Predicate device : 510(k) Number K223930 Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector A1717MCW

evidence.claim: Clinical image evaluation was performed by Wonho Chang, a radiologist.
The reviewer was deemed qualified based on clinical background, board certification in radiology, and diagnostic imaging experience.
evidence.evidenceText: Based on the reviewer’s extensive clinical background, board certification in radiology, and long-standing experience in diagnostic imaging, the reviewer is deemed fully qualified to assess the clinical performance of the subject device.

evidence.claim: The total average annotation score was 3.80 for A1717MCW and 3.94 for G1717CW.
The report conclusion states that G1717CW had slightly better image quality and appeared to have more diagnostic value.
evidence.evidenceText: the image taken with G1717CW is a little better image quality such as spatial resolution and the difference between two substance. In conclusion,G1717CW seems to be more of a diagnostic value.

evidence.claim: The imaging software supports DICOM 3.0 for image formats, transmission, and storage.
evidence.evidenceText: Image formats, transmission, and storage supported by the DICOM 3.0 standard

reviewFlags.reason: Prepared, review, and approval signature dates are blank in the source text.
Test date and clinical image evaluation date fields are present but blank in the test information table.
Authority is inferred from source metadata, source path, and 510(k) context; the body text does not explicitly name FDA.
The source text contains OCR or formatting issues, including misspellings such as 'Patient Resister', 'Consol software', 'Imagsse', and 'DCIOM 3.0'.
Knee AP section shows inconsistent product headings and evaluation columns, including HAD1417MCW and F1417MCW despite the main comparison being A1717MCW and G1717CW.
Source path references K252912, while the document body identifies predicate 510(k) Number K223930; no submission number for the subject device is stated in the body.
